mathematical_model: PPP-B2b orbit correction recovery: precise orbit = broadcast orbit + correction vector in ECEF computed from radial, along-track, and cross-track components; earthquake magnitude scaling law: log₀(PGD) = A + B·M_W + C·log₀(R), where PGD is derived from real-time PPP-B2b displacement series.
correction_types: orbit corrections (radial, along-track, cross-track) and clock corrections
reason: The paper evaluates PPP-B2b service for real-time earthquake monitoring, a specific downstream application of BDS-3 corrections.
novelty_audit: The paper presents a first assessment of the BDS-3 PPP-B2b satellite-based real-time service for earthquake source description, offering a globally available, internet-free alternative to terrestrial real-time precise positioning systems.
metrics: Accuracy of PPP-B2b derived coseismic displacement time series (RMS in north, east, up components)
Earthquake magnitude estimated via peak ground displacement (PGD) scaling law and difference from catalog magnitude
Focal mechanism parameters (strike, dip, rake) compared to reference solutions (USGS, CENC)
main_results: PPP-B2b service can provide near-real-time coseismic displacement waveforms with sufficient accuracy; the PGD-based magnitude estimate for the 2021 Mw 7.4 Maduo earthquake closely matches published values; preliminary focal mechanism inversion results are consistent with reference mechanisms.
datasets: 1-Hz GNSS observations (GPS+BDS) from regional continuous stations around the Maduo earthquake epicenter; BDS-3 PPP-B2b real-time orbit and clock corrections broadcast via GEO satellite; reference earthquake catalogs (USGS, CENC).
